title: Nuclei Isolation from Human Brain Using Sucrose Gradient
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9gg6q","text":"This protocol outlines our preparation of single-nuclei suspension from surgically acquired fresh human adult brain tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare lysis buffer and sucrose solution: %s %s Lysis buffer 30 ml Final 2M Sucrose 4.8 ml 0.32 M 1M CaCl2 150 μl 5 mM 1M Mg(Ac)2 90 μl 3 mM 0.5M EDTA 6 μl 0.1 mM 1M Tris-HCI(pH8.0) 300 μl 10 mM 0.1M PMSF 30 μL 0.1 mM 100% Triton X-100 30 μL 0.10% 100% NP-40 30 μL 0.10% Protease inhibitor 300 μL 1X RNase inhibitor 150 μL 0.1 U/μL Molecular biology grade (MBG) water 24.26 ml %s %s Sucrose solution 50 ml Final 2M sucrose 45 ml 1.8 M 1M Mg(Ac)2 150 μl 3 mM 1M Tris-HCI(pH8.0) 500 μl 10 mM MBG water 4.35 ml
Prepare Nuclei Suspension Buffer (NSB) For drop-seq: 1x PBS, 0.01% BSA (ultra pure), 0.2U/ul RNAse inhibitor. For 10X Genomics: 1X PBS, 1%BSA (ultra pure), 0.2U/ul RNase inhibitor
Put ~250mg of brain tissue into a douncer with 10ml of ice-cold lysis buffer on ice.
Dounce the tissue for 2 min while on ice.
Take 18ml of sucrose solution into a new 30ml clear ultracentrifuge tube on ice.
Place the homogenized brain into ultracentrifuge tube on ice (gradient with the homogenized brain on top of the sucrose solution).
Weigh the ultracentrifuge tubes, and adjust the weight by lysis buffer.
Ultracentrifuge with SW28 rotor at 24,400RPM (=107,163.6 RCF) for 2.5h at 4°C.
Remove supernatant, add 500μL of NSB to each pellet, and incubate them on ice for 10min.
Resuspend the nuclei in NSB, filter through Flowmi cell strainer (Bel-Art, H13680-0040) and transfer the suspension into one 2ml tube on ice.
Stain 10μl of nuclei suspension with DAPI (1:5000) or 0.4% Trypan Blue to count.
Adjust the volume with NSB to 1000 nuclei/μl (for 10X Genomics) and 300 nuclei/μl (for drop-seq).
creator: Fatma Ayhan
